observations.recommendation:  We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to: (a) continue its efforts in complying with the COVID-19 immunization program; (b) strengthen its campaign and widen the scope of its information drive to generate more demand for the vaccine and continue its vaccination roll out until the desired local herd immunity is attained; and (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer to determine and recognize in the books the full cost of the ending inventory of vaccines and ancillary supplies as at year end and to henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same.

2.7. We reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to (a) instruct the (i) Municipal Treasurer to adopt effective cash management strategies with the end view of gradually reducing the cash deficits without sacrificing the funding of current operations; and (ii) OIC-Municipal Accountant to regularly monitor the status of trust funds vis-à-vis cash balances at the end of every reporting period; and (b) continue adopting cost-cutting and revenue-generating schemes to improve the cash position of the Municipality.

3.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) adopt appropriate and legal remedies to enforce the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees, as applicable; and (b) continue to strictly observe the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, liquidation and utilization of cash advances.

4.23. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant in coordination with the PaMES Administrator to (i) recommend for the formulation of a policy on the provision for the impairment of Accounts Receivables based on collectability and other factors such as age of accounts, collection experiences, and identified doubtful accounts, among others; (ii) require the personnel concerned to regularly submit the aging schedule of accounts receivable to serve as basis in the computation of allowance for impairment; and (iii) determine the cause of the noted discrepancy in the records of both offices, and adjust records as necessary; (b) PaMES Administrator to appropriately implement service disconnections for overdue customers; (c) PaMES Board of Directors to promulgate operational manual/policy for PaMES in order to have a systematic and uniform procedure on the billing, collection and on the overall operation of the enterprise; and (d) Municipal Accountant and PaMES administrator to update and reconcile the subsidiary ledger of the receivable accounts of the PaMES and exert effort in determining the discrepancy of ₱54,094,252.26 made a necessary adjustment thereof.

5.9. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to maintain SLCs using the Perpetual Inventory Method for procured supplies and materials and prepare necessary adjusting entries on the affected accounts; (b) Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain inventory records and reports such as SSMI and SCs; and (c) Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of supplies and materials semestrally and to determine the cost of supplies and materials that will be restored back to inventory accounts as at end of the reporting period.

6.7 We reiterate our recommendations and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Inventory Committee to complete its mandated function of conducting a physical count of the LGU's assets and to submit the corresponding report; (b) Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to exert more efforts in locating documents pertinent to the acquisition of its PPEs, or consider the option for a one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts provided COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) reclassify the completed projects from the CIP account to the appropriate PPE accounts, as warranted; and (ii) effect the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the accumulated depreciation of the completed assets; and (d) Human Resource Management Officer to take appropriate action on how to address the inadequacy of staff at the Property Office.

7.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) continue the effective and efficient implementation of the projects; and (b) instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time.
8.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to maintain its satisfactory compliance with the existing rule on the appropriation and utilization of the 5% LDRRMF.

9.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal Engineer and BAC to strictly observe the guidelines provided in the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects specifically in the preparation of ABC of future projects, and (b) Municipal Engineer, BAC and the Technical Working Group explain why they should not refund the ₱280,809.12 overstatement of the project cost.

10.11. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to (a) coordinate with concerned offices throughout the processes of GAD planning and reporting of accomplishments; (b) use the HGDG tool in the attribution of PPA costs; and (c) maximize the use of GAD fund by giving appropriate consideration of the on-going pandemic in the identification of gender-responsive PPAs during next years’ GAD planning.

11.7. We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue in (a) complying with the regulations set by the BIR regarding the withholding of taxes; and (b) settling the Municipality’s tax obligations of prior years.

12.7. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) continue complying with the remittance of the mandatory deductions within the prescribed period; and (b) prioritize the payments of the monthly amortization of obligations under the MOA to avoid further interest and penalties and suspension of rights and benefits of the employees.
13.3. We recommended and the LCE agreed to maintain compliance with R.A. No. 9679, or the 2009 Home Development Mutual Fund Law.

14.5. We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue deducting and remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation; and (b) make preliminary discussions with the PhilHealth in the possible restructuring of the Municipality’s unsettled accounts.

15.3. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require all those named responsible and liable for the Notices of Suspension and Disallowance, respectively, to comply immediately with the requirements stated in the said Notices.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the:

(a) Inventory Committee to do its mandated function of conducting the physical count of the assets of the LGU and to render the corresponding report;


(b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, as the Acting Property Officer, to:
(i) exert more efforts in locating documents pertinent to the acquisition of its PPEs, or consider the option for a one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts provided COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;

(ii) maintain their respective PPE records; and



(iii) reconcile their records at least on a quarterly basis; and

(c) Human Resource Management Officer to take appropriate action on how to address the inadequacy of staff at the Property Office.
observations.managementAction:
The newly assigned Property Custodian immediately began conducting physical inventories of all departments for validation of Inventory Committee.


The Accounting Office has already created a Property Ledger Card and track available records pertaining to the acquisition of PPE.


The respective offices have been directed to keep accurate records of PPE.

The respective offices made a commitment to reconcile their records.

The LCE already issued an executive Order to augment the staff at the Property Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

(a) instruct the:

(i) OIC-Municipal Treasurer to adopt effective cash management strategies with the end view of gradually reducing the cash deficits without sacrificing the funding of current operations; and


(ii) Municipal Accountant to regularly monitor the status of trust funds vis-à-vis cash balances at the end of every reporting period; and

(b) continue adopting cost-cutting and revenue-generating schemes to improve the cash position of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU implemented effective cash management strategies in order to meet prior year obligations without jeopardizing current priority operations, particularly those pertaining to the Constituents' welfare.

The Municipal Accountant regularly monitored the status of funds and cash balance.

The LGU implemented a new revenue code to improve collection and began prioritizing where to spend the funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

(a) adopt appropriate and legal remedies to enforce the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees, as applicable; and

(b) continue to strictly observe the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, liquidation and utilization of cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Demand letters were already prepared.


The Municipality strictly implemented the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:

(a) Municipal Accountant and PAMES administrator to exert best efforts to update and complete the subsidiary ledger of the receivable accounts of the PAMES;

(b) PAMES administrator to send demand letters to delinquent customers; and


(c) PAMES Board of Directors to promulgate operational manual/policy for PAMES in order to have a systematic and uniform procedure on the billing, collection and on overall operation of the enterprise.
observations.managementAction:
PAMES personnel are currently updating their customers' subsidiary ledgers.


The municipality issued demand letters to delinquent customers.

The PAMES Board of Directors held 2 Board meetings
and is currently finalizing the operational manual/policies of
PAMES.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive concurred to instruct the 

(a) Municipal Engineer to ensure that the 16 unimplemented projects as of year-end are completed as planned; and

(b) Local Development Council to conform planned PPAs to the forecasted financial capability of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The municipality completed 14 PPAs while the remaining two are partially acted upon.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the LDRRM Council together with the LDRRM Officer to: properly plan and monitor the implementation of the PPAs for 5% LDRRMF as well as maximize the use of the fund in order to achieve the objectives in disaster risk reduction and mitigation.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Council and Officer maximized Fund utilization and ensured that PPAs were implemented on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive concurred that the GAD Focal Point System, in coordination with the Local Planning Development Office to:

(a) create a GAD database to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data and use the same in GAD planning, programming and policy making; and 


(b) timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report; and 

(c) henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD Focal Person began consolidating data in preparation for the creation of an updated GAD database and held regular consultation meetings with the relevant offices/departments to discuss the status of GAD programs and activities.

The GAD Focal Person submitted the GAD accomplishment report within the specified time frame.

The management abides by the terms of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive concurred to instruct the:

(a) Accountant to properly account all BGCM transactions; and 

(b) BAC Secretariat to post the required procurement data for all emergency purchases under the R.A. No. 11469 to promote transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction:
All BGCM related transactions have been properly accounted for by the Municipal Accountant.


All COVID-19-related transactions have been properly accounted for by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the:  

(a) MSWDO to:

(i) fully examine the master list of SAP beneficiaries against the SAP payroll to detect erroneous payment and/or documentary deficiencies; and 

(ii) call the payees concerned to explain the differences noted in the specimen signatures and inconsistencies in personal information, and take appropriate actions to ensure that the SAP fund was used as intended.
observations.managementAction:
The MSWDO has already verified the Master list of SAP beneficiaries in collaboration with the Barangay Captains.

The MSWDO's head and staff are currently comparing and verifying SAP beneficiaries' specimen signatures against payroll records.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Management in their satisfactory compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-09 and in ensuring transparency and accountability on the in-kind donations received by the Municipality by accounting all donations received during quarantine period.
observations.managementAction: Management continues to adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-09.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to continue in:

(a) complying with the regulations set by the BIR regarding the withholding of taxes; and 

(b) settling the Municipality’s tax obligations of prior years.
observations.managementAction:
All withholding taxes and VAT retained by the LGU were remitted within the specified time period.

The LGU made quarterly payments on previous year's obligations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: 

(a) continue to comply with the remittance of the mandatory deductions within the prescribed period; and 

(b) prioritize the payments of the monthly amortization of obligations under the MOA to avoid further interest and penalties and suspension of rights and benefits of the employees.
observations.managementAction:
All withheld contributions were remitted within the prescribed time.

Monthly amortization was paid on time by the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue deducting and remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation; and 

(b) make preliminary discussions with the Philhealth in the possible restructuring of the Municipality’s unsettled accounts.
observations.managementAction:
All withheld contributions were remitted within the prescribed time.

The LGU has communicated with Philhealth regarding the possibility of restructuring the Municipality's unsettled accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality continuously remit to HDMF all premium contributions and loan payments made by employees, in accordance with RA No. 9679's requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require all those named responsible and liable for the Notices of Suspension and Disallowance, respectively, to comply immediately with the requirements stated in the said Notices.
observations.managementAction: The LGU communicated with the concerned employees and officials in order to complete and submit the required papers and documents.

observations.reason:
Not implemented



Not implemented



Not implemented

Not implemented




The Municipal Treasurer’s Office already started the inventory count of the properties during the first quarter of CY 2020, however, it was temporarily suspended due to the pandemic. Moreover, the concerned personnel admitted that she cannot finish the inventory taking of properties because she has other tasks within the MTO. This is
reiterated as Finding No. 1 in Part II of this report.

The HRMO disclosed that they are already in the process of creating a General Services Officer in order to focus of the supply and property management of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) submit to the Municipal Accountant an updated and complete duly certified list of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year 2019 and every year thereafter; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to 
(i) set up RPT and SET Receivables for each year corresponding to the lists and make necessary adjustments as warranted; and 

(ii) henceforth observe the prescribed accounting procedures in recording RPT and SET receivable and deferred income accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the:
 
(a) Municipal Engineering and Accounting Offices to promptly conduct an inventory on the LGU’s local road and road network system;

(b)  Municipal Engineering Office to designate personnel that will maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and 

(c) the Municipal Accountant to trace records of prior years’ completed infrastructure projects which were closed to government equity and restore the same in the books in accordance with the transition provision.

We reiterate our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: 
(a) instruct the
 
OIC-Municipal Treasurer to adopt effective cash management strategies with the end view of gradually reducing the cash deficits without sacrificing the current operations; and 

(ii) Municipal Accountant to regularly monitor the status of trust funds vis-à-vis cash balances at the end of every reporting period; and 

(b) continue adopting cost-cutting and revenue-generating schemes to improve the cash position of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
 
(a) adopt all appropriate and legal remedies to enforce the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees, as applicable; and 

(b) continue to strictly observe the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, liquidation and utilization of cash advances.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to 

(a) continue its efforts to substantially comply with the guidelines set forth by R.A. No. 10121 and minimum standards as enumerated under the Disaster Preparedness Volume 2; and 

(b) require the concerned MDRRM Officer to comply with the reporting guidelines on the monthly preparation and regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineering Office, the Bids and Awards Committee and the project proponent offices to coordinate in order to 

(a) ensure that foreseeable issues that may hinder the implementation of the projects are adequately considered in planning;

(b) conduct the timely procurement of the PPAs; and 



(c) Identify delays in project implementation and introduce timely interventions.
observations.reason: Not implemented – As already noted in past audits, the Municipality had been operating on the negative whereby cash and cash equivalents were not sufficient to pay the current liabilities. Thus, implementation of current PPAs was always dependent on whether enough cash had been collected to sustain project implementation, i.e., after the more prioritized trust obligations are provided for.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to 

(a) strictly implement the No Segregation No Collection Policy and impose sanctions on noncompliance, if necessary; and

(b) complete the membership of the MSWMB.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
 
(a) direct the GAD Focal Point System to do proper coordination to fully utilize the GAD funds in order to attain the desired benefits that could be derived therefrom; and
 

(b) submit to the Audit Team the GAD accomplishment Report on time.
observations.reason: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: 

(a) continue to comply with the regulations set by the BIR regarding the withholding of taxes; and

(b) gradually settle the Municipality’s tax obligations incurred in previous years.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office to 

(a) continue to comply with the remittance of the mandatory deductions within the prescribed period; 

(b) prioritize the payments of the monthly amortization of obligations under the MOA to avoid further interest and penalties and suspension of rights and benefits of the employees; and 

(c) ensure that trust funds are used solely for purposes for which the fund is received or established.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to

(a) continue deducting and remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation; and 
 
(b) reconcile their records with the PhilHealth and prioritize the remittance of contributions thereto.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the:

(a) concerned office or Inventory Committee, to complete the physical count of the assets of the LGU and to render the corresponding report; 

(b) MTO to maintain and update records on accountability on PPEs; and

(c) reconciliation of between records of MTO and Municipal Accountant should be regularly done and effect the necessary adjustment to correct the reflected balances, if any.
observations.reason:
Management justified that lack of time and manpower hinders them to complete the task. This is
reiterated as Finding No. 1 in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant: 

(a) to locate the missing records and subsidiary ledgers to support the balances of obligations reflected in the Financial Statements; and 

(b) to update and reconcile the records on file to comply with the provisions under Sections 111 and 114 of the P.D. 1445.
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Accountant already exerted her best effort to locate the documents but her efforts were found in vain.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and Market Supervisor:

(a) send out letters to market lessees requesting for the updating of the rental payments on market stalls occupied by them; and 




(b) remind the lessees of the provisions of the Market Code of Pantabangan 2008, particularly in paying the rentals on a timely manner and the consequences of not paying within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The MTO is waiting for the approval of the new Market Code. Moreover, the update of the lessees is not yet completed. He justified that lack of time and personnel hindered them to accomplish the recommendation. They however committed that the recommendations will be complied in 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Inventory Committee to once and for all complete the physical count of the assets of the LGU and to render the corresponding report; (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, as the Acting Property Officer, to (i) coordinate in locating documents pertinent to the acquisition of its PPEs; (ii) maintain their respective PPE records; and (iii) reconcile their records at least on a quarterly basis; and (c) Human Resource Management Officer to take appropriate action on how to address the inadequacy of staff at the Property Office.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) submit to the Municipal Accountant an updated and complete duly certified list of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year 2019 and every year thereafter; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) set up RPT and SET Receivables for each year corresponding to the lists and make necessary adjustments as warranted; and (ii) henceforth observe the prescribed accounting procedures in recording RPT and SET receivable and deferred income accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal Engineering and Accounting Offices to promptly conduct an inventory on the LGU’s local road and road network system (b)  Municipal Engineering Office to designate personnel that will maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and (c) the Municipal Accountant to trace records of prior years’ completed infrastructure projects which were closed to government equity and restore the same in the books in accordance with the transition provision.

We reiterate our recommendation  and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the (i) OIC-Municipal Treasurer to adopt effective cash management strategies with the end view of gradually reducing the cash deficits without sacrificing the current operations; and (ii) Municipal Accountant to regularly monitor the status of trust funds vis-à-vis cash balances at the end of every reporting period; and (b) continue adopting cost-cutting and revenue-generating schemes to improve the cash position of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) adopt all appropriate and legal remedies to enforce the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees, as applicable; and (b) continue to strictly observe the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, liquidation and utilization of cash advances.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) continue its efforts to substantially comply with the guidelines set forth by R.A. No. 10121 and minimum standards as enumerated under the Disaster Preparedness Volume 2; and (b) require the concerned MDRRM Officer to comply with the reporting guidelines on the monthly preparation and regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineering Office, the Bids and Awards Committee and the project proponent offices to coordinate in order to (a) ensure that foreseeable issues that may hinder the implementation of the projects are adequately considered in planning; (b) conduct the timely procurement of the PPAs; and (c) identify delays in project implementation and introduce timely interventions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) strictly implement the No Segregation No Collection Policy and impose sanctions on noncompliance, if necessary; and (b) complete the membership of the MSWMB.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) direct the GAD Focal Point System to do proper coordination to fully utilize the GAD funds in order to attain the desired benefits that could be derived therefrom; and (b) submit to the Audit Team the GAD accomplishment Report on time.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) continue to comply with the regulations set by the BIR regarding the withholding of taxes; and (b) gradually settle the Municipality’s tax obligations incurred in previous years.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office to (a) continue to comply with the remittance of the mandatory deductions within the prescribed period; (b) prioritize the payments of the monthly amortization of obligations under the MOA to avoid further interest and penalties and suspension of rights and benefits of the employees; and (c) ensure that trust funds are used solely for purposes for which the fund is received or established.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) continue deducting and remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation; and (b) reconcile their records with the PhilHealth and prioritize the remittance of contributions thereto.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require all those named responsible and liable for the Notices of Suspension and Disallowance, respectively, to comply immediately with the requirements stated in the said Notices.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE instruct the:

(a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) update and complete the Certified List of Taxpayers that shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables for CY 2019; and 

(ii) prepare the list of delinquencies as at end of CY 2018 and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the updating of individual tax payer’s account.  

Meanwhile, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor act on the reported inadequacy of personnel at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the posting and updating of the RPTAR.
observations.managementAction:
MTO started to reconcile the Certified List of Taxpayers.


MTO prepared the List of Delinquencies and started updating the individual tax payers’ account.

Management was advised to fully utilize the Job Orders hired by the municipality.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation and listing of taxpayer was not completely done as of to date, as such, the amount set-up as RPT/SET Receivables are incomplete as of year-end.
Reiterated in Observation No. 2, Part II, this report

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation and management agreed that the LCE would instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to: 

(a) stop the practice of using cash reserve for trust liabilities for payment of regular expenditures and to promptly remit trust funds due to other government agencies; 

(b) control disbursements based on available funds/appropriation and not on available cash balances; and 

(c) ensure that Trust fund account are made available only for the specific expenditures for which it was created or received.  

Moreover, we advise the Local Chief Executive to continue: 
(a) adopting cost-cuttings and; 

(b) enhancing revenue generating strategies to improve the cash position of the Municipality.

We reiterate our recommendation and management concurred that the LCE will require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

(a) coordinate with the concerned offices for the proper and complete documentation of the said payables; 

(b) consider reverting to government equity the recorded payables which are more than two years and not supported with documents or details; and

(c) ensure that the payables recorded are adequately and properly documented.

We recommended and the management agreed that the incumbent Local Chief Executive: 

(a) adopt all legal and appropriate remedies compelling the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees and/or file appropriate charges against them, if warranted; and 


(b) continue to impose the strict observance of the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, liquidation and utilization of cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Management tried to communicate with the officials and employees, as well as those who already retired/ resigned.

For CY 2018, management was able to impose these provisions.
observations.reason:
Lack of proper liquidation documents; 
Reiterated in Observation No. 4, Part II, this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to :

instruct the MDRRMO to facilitate the early preparation/ formulation of the LDRRMFIP and other related reports in order that the planned DRRM PPAs could be implemented to guarantee the attainment of the Municipality’s objective of mitigating the impacts of disaster/ calamities towards building a disaster-resilient communities. 

refrain from using LDRRMF for expenses which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; and 

require the concerned MDRRM Officer to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation and regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO admitted that he is not aware of the guidelines. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that priority will be given to the projects /programs/ activities under the 20% Development Fund in order to deliver the optimum public service and provide projects/programs/ activities that will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
observations.reason: Projects from continuing appropriations were already completed during the year, however, there were five projects unimplemented pertaining to current year appropriations. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the LCE will instruct the MSWMB and MENRO to: 

(a) prioritize the finalization and implementation of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification and the prompt resolution of issues/gaps; 

(b) involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws; and 

(c) intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy” among the residents.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Government Unit: 

allocate at least 5% of the total appropriations to GAD related activities/PPAs; 


(b) ensure that the expenses incurred for the programs and activities have properly authorized appropriations; and 

(c) create M&E team who will assess the overall effectiveness and efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts.
observations.reason: The GAD Focal Person failed to coordinate with the GFPS and other department heads of the Municipality.  Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive: 

(a) create an inventory to conduct an inventory of the local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the LGU; 

(b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer, as the Acting Property Officer of the Municipality to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and the complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and 

(c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) effect the necessary entries to recognize the completed road networks, 

(ii) keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and
 
(iii) prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
observations.reason:
Although the LCE instructed the MT, he still failed to act on the recommendation due to limited time and lack of manpower.


The MT did not submit an inventory of the Road Network. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to immediately verify the causes of the difference between the balances appearing in the General Ledger against the CIPLC and effect the necessary correction to reconcile the balances.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to: 

(a) coordinate with concerned employees handling the processing of documents for the construction of the Two-Storey Commercial Complex and Transport Terminal for the submission of final documentations to properly establish the accountability of the contractor and to secure the interest of the municipality; and 

(b) effect the necessary adjusting entries to properly reclassify the completed and fully paid projects under the Construction-in-Progress account to its concerned PPE accounts.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:

(a) the OIC- Human Resource Management Officer to clearly define in the contract of service the duties and responsibilities of Job Order personnel to avoid confusion and duplication of duties; and 

(b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents, such as accomplishment reports for services rendered, were attached to the disbursements vouchers to establish validity and propriety of payment.
 
Moreover, management should maximize the benefits of hiring Job Orders by properly identifying the needs of each Department so that the proper number of Job Orders possessing the right qualifications could be established.

observations.reason: The contract of lease will be drawn once the new Market Code is passed. The management informed that it is scheduled to be deliberated in 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to:

(a) continue to comply with the regulations set by the BIR regarding the withholding of taxes; and

(b) settle the Municipality’s tax obligations incurred in previous years.
observations.reason: The management is gradually settling the previous years’ tax due.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office to:
 
(a) continue to comply with the remittance of the mandatory deductions within the prescribed period; 



(b) prioritize the payments of the monthly amortization of obligations under the MOA to avoid further interest and penalties and suspension of rights and benefits of the employees; and 


(c) ensure that trust funds are used solely for purposes for which the fund is received or established.
observations.managementAction:
For CYs 2017 to 2019, withheld contributions were remitted within the prescribed period.

The Management started to make partial settlements of the previous years’ balance in compliance with their MOA with GSIS

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the LCE require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
(a) continue deducting and remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation; and 

(b) reconcile their records with the PhilHealth and prioritize the remittance of contributions thereto.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) concerned office or Inventory Committee, to complete the physical count of the assets of the LGU and to render the corresponding report; (b) Municipal Treasurer, as the Acting Property Officer, to maintain and update records on accountability on PPEs; and (c) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records regularly and effect the necessary adjustment, if any.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) update and complete the Certified List of Taxpayers that shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables for CY 2019; and (b) prepare the list of delinquencies as at end of CY 2018 and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the updating of individual tax payer’s account.  Meanwhile, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor act on the reported inadequacy of personnel at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the posting and updating of the RPTAR.

We reiterate our recommendation  and management agreed that the LCE (a) instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to (i) stop the practice of using cash reserved for trust liabilities in paying regular expenditures and to promptly remit trust funds due to other government agencies; (ii) control disbursements based on available funds/appropriation and not on available cash balances; and (iii) ensure that Trust fund account are made available only for the specific expenditures for which the trust fund was created or received; and (b) continue adopting cost-cuttings and enhancing revenue generating strategies to improve the cash position of the Municipality.

We reiterate our recommendation and management agreed that the LCE require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) coordinate with the concerned offices for the proper and complete documentation of the said payables; (b) consider reverting to government equity the recorded payables which are more than two years have no valid claimants and not supported with documents or details; and (c) ensure that the payables recorded are adequately and properly documented.

We recommended and the management agreed that the incumbent Local Chief Executive (a) adopt all legal and appropriate remedies compelling the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees and/or file appropriate charges against them, if warranted; and (b) continue to impose the strict observance of the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, liquidation and utilization of cash advances.

We recommended and the management agreed to –
instruct the MDRRMO to facilitate the early preparation/formulation of the LDRRMFIP and other related reports in order that the planned DRRM PPAs could be implemented to guarantee the attainment of the Municipality’s objective of mitigating the impacts of disaster/calamities towards building a disaster-resilient communities.
refrain from using LDRRMF for expenses which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; and
require the concerned MDRRM Officer to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation and regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation.

We recommended and management agreed that priority will be given to the projects/programs/activities under the 20% Development Fund in order to deliver the optimum public service and provide projects/programs/ activities that will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive a) instruct the MSWMB to (i) review thoroughly the MSWMP; (ii) create specific plan/target for the ensuing years together with its guidelines and mechanics; and (iii) involve closely all the Barangay Officials in the strict implementation of the existing environmental law; b) complete the membership of the MSWMB; (c) follow up on the status of the approval of the new ordinance on solid waste; and (d) consider other waste disposal strategies if the contract with the MCWMC will not be ratified by the SB.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Government Unit (a) allocate at least 5% of the total appropriations to GAD related activities/PPAs; (b) ensure that the expenses incurred for the programs and activities have properly authorized appropriations; and (c) create M&E team who will assess the overall effectiveness and efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive: (a) create an inventory to conduct an inventory of the local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the LGU; (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer, as the Acting Property Officer of the Municipality to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and the complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) effect the necessary entries to recognize the completed road networks, (ii) keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and (iii) prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

We recommended and management agreed to immediately verify the causes of the difference between the balances appearing in the General Ledger against the CIPLC and effect the necessary correction to reconcile the balances.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) coordinate with concerned employees handling the processing of documents for the construction of the Two-Storey Commercial Complex and Transport Terminal for the submission of final documentations to properly establish the accountability of the contractor and to secure the interest of the municipality; and (b) effect the necessary adjusting entries to properly reclassify the completed and fully paid projects under the Construction-in-Progress account to its concerned PPE accounts.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant (a) to locate the missing records and subsidiary ledgers to support the balances of obligations reflected in the Financial Statements; and (b) to update and reconcile the records on file to comply with the provisions under Sections 111 and 114 of the P.D. 1445.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) the OIC- Human Resource Management Officer to clearly define in the contract of service the duties and responsibilities of Job Order personnel to avoid confusion and duplication of duties; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents, such as accomplishment reports for services rendered, were attached to the disbursements vouchers to establish validity and propriety of payment.  Moreover, management should maximize the benefits of hiring Job Orders by properly identifying the needs of each Department so that the proper number of Job Orders possessing the right qualifications could be established.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) draw the lease contract for all existing market stallholders; (b) fast track the approval of the New Revenue Code of Pantabangan by the Sangguniang Bayan so that they could finally apply the new competitive rates, hence, increasing their income from rentals from market stalls.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and Market Supervisor (a) send out letters to market lessees requesting for the updating of the rental payments on market stalls occupied by them; and (b) remind the lessees of the provisions of the Market Code of Pantabangan 2008, particularly in paying the rentals on a timely manner and the consequences of not paying within the prescribed period.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) continue to comply with the regulations set by the BIR regarding the withholding of taxes; and (b) settle the Municipality’s tax obligations incurred in previous years.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office to (a) continue to comply with the remittance of the mandatory deductions within the prescribed period; (b) prioritize the payments of the monthly amortization of obligations under the MOA to avoid further interest and penalties and suspension of rights and benefits of the employees; and (c) ensure that trust funds are used solely for purposes for which the fund is received or established.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) continue deducting and remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation; and (b) reconcile their records with the PhilHealth and prioritize the remittance of contributions thereto.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) require all those named responsible and liable for the Notices of Suspension and Disallowance, respectively, to comply immediately with the requirements stated in the said Notices; and (b) present for verification the alleged submitted documents pertaining to the ₱18,015,585.28 disallowed transactions.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the:

(a) concerned office or Inventory Committee, if any, to complete the physical count of the assets of the LGU and to render the corresponding report; 


(b) Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) and Accounting Office to update and regularly reconcile their property records; and 

(c) MTO to maintain and update records on accountability on PPEs.  

Moreover, we advise Management to: 

(a) address the reported inadequacy of personnel to facilitate the physical count of and reporting on PPEs; and 


(b) require proper turnover of accountabilities in case of change of accountable officers.

Management will comply in the future.

MTO was not able to update their records. Hence, no reconciliation could be done.

Meanwhile, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor act on the reported inadequacy of personnel at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the posting and updating of the RPTAR.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) continue deducting and remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation; and (b) reconcile their records with the PhilHealth and prioritize the remittance of contributions thereto.

We recommended and management agreed to continue the intensified tax collection in their collection process in Tax Revenue in order to have an additional fund that will aid them in providing greater service to its constituents.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require all those named responsible and liable for the Notices of Suspension and Disallowance, respectfully, to comply immediately with the requirements stated in the Notice of Suspensions and to present for verification the alleged submitted documents pertaining to the P18,015,585.28 disallowed transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the incumbent Local Chief Executive:
adopt all legal and appropriate remedies compelling the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees enumerated in Annex A hereof and/or file appropriate charges against them, if warranted; and
impose the strict observance of the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February  10, 1997 on the granting, liquidation and utilization of cash advances

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive :
require Ms. Gonzales to account and return  the missing funds amounting to ₱1,054,340.44
require written explanation from Ms. Gonzales on why she did not deposit/transfer the amount withdrawn to the recipient fund.
relieve  the concern accountable officer from holding position involving money and property accountability; and
file appropriate criminal or administrative charges against the erring accountable official, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
control the expenses of the agency by adopting cost-cuttings and revenue enhancing strategies to improve cash position of the municipality;
ensure that the agency is adhering strictly to the regulations relevant to budgeting and disbursement of government funds; and
instruct the Accounting Office to  exercise its pre-audit function in order to ensure that only valid claims are recorded in the books of accounts of the agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) coordinate with the concerned offices for the proper and complete documentation of the said payables; (b) consider reverting the recorded payables which are more than two years and unsupported with documents or details; and (c) ensure that the payables recorded are adequately and properly documented.

We recommended that management utilize the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 to contribute in the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management for the benefits of the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the municipality  (a) designate an accessible place or warehouse where the stand by relief goods can be safely stored/kept and to avoid possible damage/spoilage and/or loss/pilferage, and (b) refrain from purchasing perishable goods which could not be stored for a long period of time to avoid wastage of resources.

We recommended that the MSWMB and MENRO involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation of the existing environmental laws; intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents; and exercise strong political will to implement the laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended that:
management stop the practice of paying expenditures which are not included in the priority expenses chargeable against SEF as provided under Section 1 of R.A. 5447, Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and Deped-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, No. 01-A, March 14, 2000, No. 01-B, dated June 25, 2001, to avoid disallowance in audit;
the Accountant ensure that disbursement vouchers should be completely supported with valid and proper documents; and
avoid the grants of financial assistance in the form of cash for sports and development activities, instead subject the transactions to regular procurement process if the requirements compose of goods, materials or supplies.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group implementation of GAD activities in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget and adhere  strictly with provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommended that the members of the SB consider:
the enactment of appropriate ordinance to prevent undesirable outcome of unregulated proliferation of tricycles along the national highways, which may pose hazards and great danger to pedestrians and motorists’ lives and properties; and
the provision of separate lanes in the national highway for three-wheeled public conveyances in coordination with proper government authorities.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office to:
reconcile the records pertaining to GSIS contributions withheld and remitted;
identify the personnel whose GSIS contributions in the previous years were still not remitted to GSIS and effect immediate remittance thereof in compliance with the aforecited regulations; and
observe strictly the provisions of Sec. 13.1 and Section 14.1 of RA No. 8291 in the collection and remittance of all contributions as well as loan amortizations to prevent the imposition of interest on delayed remittances and the suspension of all loan privileges of any its employees as well as the disqualification to receive the yearly dividends as set forth in Section 15 and Section 16 of RA No. 8291.

We recommended that management continue to comply with the regulations set by the BIR regarding the withholding of taxes and settle the Municipality’s tax obligations incurred in previous years.
We also recommended to file appropriate charges to the Local Chief Executive’s whose terms the moneys for trust liabilities were misappropriated

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require all those named liable persons in the Notice of Disallowance to refund the audit disallowances and to comply immediately with the requirements stated in the Notice of Suspensions within the prescribed period to avoid audit disallowances.
